calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Caldwell for Linda Neal. Dennis Vann for and with Linda Harrington and Kelly Dearman.

The Parties explained circumstances and what their understanding was of the meeting that took place when the cashiers check was turned over to Ms. Harrington. The Parties also presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the issue if interest being paid on statutory fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-03-2014 Continued To Jun-10-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Continued To Jun-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Caldwell for Linda Neal. Dennis Vann for Linda Harrington and Kelly Dearman.

Ms. Caldwell stated that she obtained additional waivers and receipts for the additional interest, that they reached an agreement for payment to Ms. Dearman for a total of $6,606.11 ($3,666.67 principal plus $2,939.44 in interest at a rate of 6.5% in the amount of $238.33 per year for 12 years and 4 months) and that she will pay it within 10 days. The Court informed Ms. Caldwell that she ought to have consents from the Beneficiaries to pay the interest. Ms. Caldwell once again raised the issue of having given a cashiers check to Ms. Harrington for payment of principal for both Ms. Harrington's fees and Ms. Dearman's fees, and stated that Ms. Harrington refused to give back the money and that Ms. Harrington only joined the matter for fees. The accounting petition was continued to June 3, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated/informed Mr. Vann that Ms. Dearman and Ms. Harrington need to be present.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-06-2014 Continued To May-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-96-266340 The Estate of Nicie Lee Bright and PES-00-280962 The Estate of Bessie Ann Jackson.

Appearances: Karen Caldwell for and with Linda Neal. Dennis Vann for Kelly Dearman and Linda Harrington.

Ms. Caldwell provided endorsed copies of the receipts on distribution of the pecuniary gifts, stated that she can address the issue of the difference in the amount of interest paid (6% versus 7%) and also provided endorsed copies of the receipts for preliminary distributions to the residual Beneficiaries. Ms. Caldwell also stated that Attorney Mary Jane Gordon made an agreement to not participate in the statutory fees, that Attorney John Lyons is now deceased, that Mr. Lyons Wife agreed to waive fees, that the other residual Beneficiaries signed waivers of notice of the declaration requesting instructions and that waivers of the accounting were filed today. Furthermore, Ms. Caldwell stated that they paid the principal of Ms. Dearman and Ms. Harrington's fees, that there is a question about Ms. Harrington's request of 10% interest and that only one check was sent to Ms. Dearman. Mr. Vann stated that is is his understanding that fees were paid to Ms. Harrington and that Ms. Dearman's fees were not. In addition, Ms. Caldwell stated that the payment to Ms. Dillard was for property taxes and repairs to the stairs for the Courtland Street property, and that she filed the receipts and has consents from all of the Beneficiaries. The Parties stepped out of the courtroom to discuss what is owed to Ms. Dearman and Ms. Harrington. Status of the reserve and blocked account, and the petition for final distribution were continued to May 20, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to discuss resolution.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Bui. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Continued To May-06-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Caldwell for Linda Neal (Petitioner).

Ms. Caldwell explained that she became ill and was unable to work for a two month period, that she is now working on a part time basis, that she had other Counsel take her cases and that she represented to other Counsel that this case was continued. Ms. Caldwell also stated that taxes have not been resolved, that there is a large capital gains in the Sister's estate, that this estate is not ready for final distribution, that there is an issue of reimbursement for repairs to the Sister's estate and that her Client knows that April 15, 2014 is a drop dead deadline. The Court expressed concern about the failures to appear, provided names of other cases in which Ms. Caldwell failed to appear and stated that these cases need her attention. Ms. Caldwell represented to the Court that her new address is pending with the State Bar and provided the Court with the new address, phone number and e-mail address. The petition for final distribution on waiver of account was continued to May 6, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for response to the Probate Examiner's notes which is to be filed by April 29, 2014.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Continued To Mar-25-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Karen Caldwell to appear on March 25, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why she has failed to appear and for failure to respond to the Probate Examiner's notes. Sanctions in the amount of $500.00 will be imposed if Ms. Caldwell fails to appear on that date. The accounting petition is also continued to that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
